2604.07788.title: PeReGrINE: Evaluating Personalized Review Fidelity with User Item Graph Context
2604.07788.paper_summary: We introduce PeReGrINE, a benchmark and evaluation framework for personalized review generation grounded in graph-structured user--item evidence. PeReGrINE restructures Amazon Reviews 2023 into a temporally consistent bipartite graph, where each target review is conditioned on bounded evidence from user history, item context, and neighborhood interactions under explicit temporal cutoffs. To represent persistent user preferences without conditioning directly on sparse raw histories, we compute a User Style Parameter that summarizes each user's linguistic and affective tendencies over prior reviews. This setup supports controlled comparison of four graph-derived retrieval settings: product-only, user-only, neighbor-only, and combined evidence. Beyond standard generation metrics, we introduce Dissonance Analysis, a macro-level evaluation framework that measures deviation from expected user style and product-level consensus. We also study visual evidence as an auxiliary context source and find that it can improve textual quality in some settings, while graph-derived evidence remains the main driver of personalization and consistency. Across product categories, PeReGrINE offers a reproducible way to study how evidence composition affects review fidelity, personalization, and grounding in retrieval-conditioned language models.
2604.07788.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹 PeReGrINE，一個基於圖結構的用戶-項目證據的個性化評審生成基準和評估框架。PeReGrINE 將 Amazon Reviews 2023 重組為一個時間一致的二部圖，其中每個目標評審都是基於來自用戶歷史、項目上下文和在明確時間截止下的鄰域互動的有限證據。為了表示持久的用戶偏好，而不直接依賴稀疏的原始歷史，我們計算了一個用戶風格參數，總結每個用戶在先前評審中的語言和情感傾向。這個設置支持四種基於圖的檢索設置的受控比較：僅產品、僅用戶、僅鄰居和綜合證據。除了標準生成指標外，我們還引入了不和諧分析，一個宏觀層面的評估框架，用於測量與預期用戶風格和產品級共識的偏差。我們還研究了視覺證據作為輔助上下文來源，發現它在某些設置中可以改善文本質量，而基於圖的證據仍然是個性化和一致性的主要驅動因素。在各個產品類別中，PeReGrINE 提供了一種可重複的方法來研究證據組成如何影響評審的真實性、個性化和在檢索條件語言模型中的基礎。
2604.07788.author: Steven Au et.al.
